transcript: Arcium is the encrypted execution engine of the internet. And my vision for Arcium is for it to become this vast, distributed, almost entirely invisible network that allows everything to be connected, allows information to travel and interact at the speed of light, all while remaining fully encrypted. And one year ago at Breakpoint 2024 in Singapore, I introduced and sort of unveiled Arcium. And within the last 12 months, we've been busy building new cryptography, inventing new stuff, implementing all of those things, onboarding builders. And the past couple of months have really been humbling for me and inspiring to see the, yeah, almost exponential growth, explosive exponential growth of the number of teams building with Arcium and working on encrypted applications on top of Solana. Um, what has happened for example is that Umbra, one of the first teams that went full all-in on Arcium, their entire tech being powered by Arcium, the encrypted supercomputer, raised the largest ICO on Solana history on MetaDAO. We've also had thousands of nodes be deployed within our testnet, hundreds of developers building applications for hackathons and deploying those applications. And so, this, this idea of Arcium being the encrypted execution engine of the internet holds entirely true for Solana. For Solana we serve as the encrypted execution engine where we are providing the big missing piece to Solana, which is privacy. But we are not just providing privacy to all of our developers and users, we are providing the final form of privacy and computing with encrypted execution. So developers are able to put encrypted data on-chain, but not just individual, isolated encrypted data, but what we call encrypted shared state. The unification of everyone's collective encrypted data and then they are able to run encrypted computations over all of this data without ever anyone seeing the underlying data. And that's what developers love and that's what we've seen with developers building on top of Arcium. And all of the feedback and usage that we've gotten over the last year has tremendously helped us shape the technology that we are building. And really the, the, the symbiotic relationship basically between Solana and Arcium becomes clear when you think about what Solana is. Solana is the internet's execution engine for public shared state. Solana is this ledger where everything happens on this one single shared state. And what attempts at privacy in the past have introduced is more of a Web2 model of execution, which is running on isolated encrypted state where secrets remain in the custody of different parties, have to remain there, cannot be shared, and there's no interaction taking place between those secrets. And that's why we are doing encrypted shared state with Arcium, where we can encrypt all data and then run encrypted computations over all of that data without having to see that data. What we've also built, um, and what I am most excited about for 2026 is C-SPL, the Confidential SPL Token Standard, which enables us to bring the final form of confidentiality to Solana. Which means that for any existing token on Solana, we can encrypt the balances and encrypt the transfer amounts and hide all of that information from the public. And the reason why I am so excited about C-SPL is because in my mind, I might not be 100% objective in that regard, but in my mind it's the most advanced token standard to ever be conceived on top of a public blockchain. Simply because we are not just enabling you to take any existing token, make it confidential, have privacy, but for the first time ever we have confidential smart token accounts. Which means that for the first time ever an on-chain program, a literal smart contract, can have confidential balances and perform confidential transfers. There's no private key associated. It's literally a smart contract on-chain that can be used to manage tokens where there's not a single human being or a single computer in the world that knows how many tokens this account holds. And this smart contract can then perform confidential transfers. And this really opens up a new design space, especially considering that the interfaces that we've designed are as close as possible to the existing public token standard. So you are literally just switching the context between public and confidential and can build anything on top of that. And so we are going live with C-SPL in Q1 of next year. And will enable developers, applications, wallets, um, dApps, all to add confidentiality to their existing stack with no complexity at all. And the design space that we are opening up with this is what we call Encrypted Capital Markets. Which sort of is the logical culmination between internet capital markets, encrypted execution, and institutional finance. Where we can have anything ranging from confidential transfers up to confidential lending, confidential markets. And then the huge unlock that we are bringing is that thanks to encrypted shared state, we have different confidential markets, but differently from what was previously possible within traditional finance, now with encrypted shared state, we can execute and settle across all of those venues within a single encrypted execution because within this encrypted execution all of those trading venues are literally lit venues. And that's a huge unlock that's only possible with Solana and Arcium and will bring the entirety of institutional finance on-chain. Now all of this is possible because within the last year we've built the Cerberus protocol, which powers Arcium at its core. That's our encrypted execution engine within the execution engine I guess. Um, and Cerberus is the first ever dishonest majority protocol out there. And what that means is that within our encrypted computations we always only require one single honest participant within this distributed computation. And that's a huge game changer considering that previously you always required an honest majority to participate. And we made that practical through some cryptographic breakthroughs. And under the hood, it's very mathematical, very complex. Um, me myself I don't understand everything. Luckily I have some of the smartest cryptographers and PhDs in my team. Um, but luckily no one needs to understand how it works because we've built abstractions on top of that. We've built Arkus, the first ever Rust compiler for encrypted computations. So as a developer, what you are doing is you utilize something like C-SPL to simply call create account, transfer, and pass an encrypted value. That's it. And your signature. Nothing more. Um, and with Arkus you write normal Rust code. It's encrypted. You don't have to worry about anything. Now, we are not just the most trustless and, um, versatile encrypted computing protocol, but we've also built the most scalable encrypted computing protocol within the last year. We beat state-of-the-art solutions by orders of magnitude. What that means in practice is that instead of being able to perform maybe 20, 30 confidential transfers per second, we can perform, based on how you measure that, tens of thousands if not millions of encrypted operations per second. And Arcium itself also excels in regards to ciphertext size. So we've designed Arcium in a way where it is as composable as possible, where you can store every single piece of encrypted confidential data directly on-chain and have it interact with the public context and you can switch between both simply because our ciphertexts, our encrypted data, are as small as possible. Everything can be stored directly on Solana. Everything can be within your on-chain program and you can build those amazing new applications within this design space that we are opening up. That's everything I wanted to share with you today. Um, let's encrypt everything and compute anything. Thank you very much.

# Major Exchange Solana Breach Shakes 2025 Crypto Market
**[MEXC Blog](https://blog.mexc.com/author/shaunt/)**November 27, 2025**[Crypto News & Updates](https://blog.mexc.com/category/news/)
## Overview: Large Solana-Based Withdrawal Detected
On 27 November 2025, a prominent South Korean cryptocurrency exchange detected unauthorized withdrawals of Solana-based assets totaling approximately 54 billion KRW (about $36 million). The irregular outflow included native SOL and a range of tokens from the Solana ecosystem. The exchange immediately suspended Solana network deposits and withdrawals and initiated emergency inspections to contain the incident.
![South Korean exchange flags $36M Solana withdrawals, suspends deposits](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns=%22http://www.w3.org/2000/svg%22%20viewBox=%220%200%20210%20140%22%3E%3C/svg%3E)
### Affected assets and scope
* SOL (Solana native token)
* USDC (Solana-based stablecoin)
* Several Solana-native tokens across DeFi and meme markets
* Stolen funds were transferred to external, unidentified wallets
The exchange disclosed wallet addresses linked to the irregular withdrawals as part of its rapid response and forensic efforts. Early analysis indicates the incident was concentrated on assets held on the Solana network, suggesting a vectored compromise rather than a cross-chain exploit.
## Immediate Response and Containment Measures
Following detection, the exchange enacted multiple containment steps:
* Suspension of all Solana network deposits and withdrawals.
* Activation of emergency inspection protocols and forensic investigations.
* Public communication to affected customers with ongoing updates through official channels.
* Disclosure of wallet addresses associated with the outflows to support blockchain tracing efforts.
According to the exchange’s public noti

# Memecoin Activity Hits New Lows as Weekly Solana DEX Volume Stays Above $18.6B
240% Rise in Tokenized Assets Signal Market Rotation
[Ario](https://solanafloor.com/author/ario)
Copy link
* Published:
* Edited:
Sentiment across crypto has turned bleak. Prices are down, liquidations are routine, and timelines are full of talk about “lower for longer”. It is easy to assume users are leaving and activity is collapsing with price.
Onchain data from the past 100 days on Solana tells a more nuanced story. Rather than a simple exodus, the picture looks like a rotation. Some speculative segments are shrinking, while others such as tokenized assets and perpetual trading are taking a larger share of the pie. The market is clearly risk-off in some areas but not switched off.
## Memecoins Lose Share, Volume Rotates Elsewhere
![Trading Vol by Category](https://sf-cms.step.finance/assets/5ad43699-2ebc-4922-b063-cd98a1c5975e.png?)The memecoin sector has seen the steepest contraction in this rotation. Over the past 100 days, memecoins on Solana DEXs have dropped from 34.8% of weekly trading volume in early August to below 9.1%, reducing their relative share to less than one-third of prior levels. The last comparable period was December 2023, when $SOL traded near $70, total weekly DEX volume was about $5.4B, and memecoins accounted for roughly $650M. Despite today’s lower share, total weekly DEX volume now exceeds $18.6B, with memecoin volume around $1.7B. This indicates not a disappearance of activity, but a recalibration of speculative demand within the Solana ecosystem.
What has not happened is an equivalent collapse in overall DEX volume. Instead of disappearing, liquidity has moved. Trading in $SOL-stablecoin pairs, project tokens and Bitcoin has grown. Bitcoin’s share of weekly volume has risen above 4.5%, and tokenized assets have seen the most dramatic change: from less than 0.08% of weekly volume in early August to more than 0.188% (240% growth) over recent weeks.
## Mid-Sized Traders Gain Ground as Tiny Tickets Decline
![Trading Vol by Size](https://sf-cms.step.finance/assets/f9737986-49ca-424c-b291-97ff085abefa.png?)Trades with volume between $1K and $10K have increased their share of DEX activity. Over the past 100 days, their contribution to weekly volume has risen from 33.2% to more than 37%. This suggests that “serious retail” or mid-sized traders remain engaged and may even be taking a larger role in providing liquidity and volume.
At the same time, trades under $1K have declined. Their share of weekly volume has fallen by ab

editorial.headline: Arcium Unveils C-SPL Standard to Bring Confidential Smart Contracts to Solana
editorial.standfirst: The new standard introduces 'Encrypted Shared State' to the network, enabling institutional-grade privacy for lending and capital markets by Q1 2026.
key_takeaways: Arcium announced the Confidential SPL (C-SPL) standard, launching Q1 2026, to enable programmable privacy for any Solana token.
Privacy protocol Umbra, built on Arcium, recently secured $155M in ICO commitments via MetaDAO, signaling massive market demand.
The network is powered by the Cerberus protocol, a 'dishonest majority' MPC system requiring only one honest node for security.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The 'transparency vs. privacy' trade-off that has long kept institutional capital on the sidelines of DeFi is effectively over. At Solana Breakpoint 2025, **Arcium** CEO Yannik Schrade unveiled the Confidential SPL (C-SPL) standard, a primitive designed to bring what he calls the "final form" of privacy to the blockchain: **Encrypted Shared State**. Unlike previous privacy solutions that isolated encrypted data in silos (preventing interaction), C-SPL allows smart contracts to hold confidential balances and execute logic over collective encrypted data without ever decrypting it. 

This distinction is critical for the emergence of **Encrypted Capital Markets**. Current blockchain privacy often mimics a Web2 model where secrets are held custodially or locally. Arcium’s approach, powered by its proprietary **Cerberus** protocol, allows for a "confidential smart token account"—a program that functions autonomously on-chain with hidden liquidity, yet remains fully composable with the rest of Solana. This unlocks the holy grail for institutional finance: on-chain dark pools, confidential payroll, and private lending markets that can settle atomically against public order books.

The demand for this infrastructure is already empirically proven. Schrade highlighted **Umbra**, a privacy protocol built entirely on Arcium, which recently shattered records by attracting $155 million in commitments during its ICO on **MetaDAO**. This massive oversubscription signals that the market is rapidly rotating toward privacy-preserving applications, provided they don't sacrifice the speed and composability Solana is known for. To support this scale, Arcium introduced **Arcis**, a Rust-based compiler that abstracts the complex mathematics of Multi-Party Computation (MPC), allowing developers to write confidential applications as easily as standard Solana programs.

Under the hood, Arcium claims a significant cryptographic breakthrough with Cerberus, a "dishonest majority" protocol. While traditional MPC networks require a majority of honest participants to secure data, Cerberus requires only a single honest node to maintain integrity. This efficiency gain allows the network to process tens of thousands of encrypted operations per second, orders of magnitude higher than the 20-30 TPS limit of legacy privacy solutions. With C-SPL scheduled for a Q1 2026 mainnet launch, Solana is positioning itself not just as a high-performance ledger, but as the primary execution layer for confidential institutional value.
